source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: Four biomarkers demonstrated perfect discrimination (AUC=1.00) between PSC-associated CCA and PSC alone in tissue samples.
finding.effect_summary: Perfect discrimination between PSC-associated CCA and PSC alone in tissue samples.

source_quote: Four biomarkers demonstrated perfect discrimination (AUC=1.00) between PSC-associated CCA (PSC-CCA) and PSC alone in tissue samples.

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: In bile, all four biomarkers achieved an AUC >0.9 for distinguishing PSC-CCA from PSC alone.
finding.effect_summary: High AUC >0.9 for distinguishing PSC-CCA from PSC alone in bile.

source_quote: In bile, all four biomarkers achieved an AUC >0.9 for distinguishing PSC-CCA (n=20) from PSC alone (n=207).

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: A two-biomarker panel (5-42952178 and PRKCB) achieved 95% sensitivity and 90% specificity in bile.
finding.effect_summary: Two-biomarker panel achieved 95% sensitivity and 90% specificity in bile.

source_quote: A two-biomarker panel (5-42952178 and PRKCB) achieved 95% sensitivity and 90% specificity.

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: Adding KCNA6 and ZFP82 increased specificity to 97% with sensitivity maintained at 90% in bile.
finding.effect_summary: Specificity increased to 97% with sensitivity at 90% by adding KCNA6 and ZFP82.

source_quote: Adding KCNA6 and ZFP82 increased specificity to 97% with sensitivity maintained at 90%.

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: The two-biomarker bile panel detected all eight CCA cases (100% sensitivity) in samples collected 3-12 months prior to diagnosis.
finding.effect_summary: 100% sensitivity in detecting CCA cases 3-12 months before diagnosis.

source_quote: The two-biomarker bile panel successfully detected all eight CCA cases (100% sensitivity) in samples collected 3-12 months prior to diagnosis by conventional methods.

source.title: Accurate detection of cholangiocarcinoma in primary sclerosing cholangitis by DNA methylation biomarkers in bile and plasma.

finding.description: In plasma, a two-biomarker panel (KCNA6 and PRKCB) achieved 70% sensitivity and 90% specificity for distinguishing PSC-CCA and PSC alone.
finding.effect_summary: 70% sensitivity and 90% specificity in plasma for distinguishing PSC-CCA and PSC alone.

source_quote: Performance was lower in plasma, where an alternative two-biomarker panel (KCNA6 and PRKCB) achieved 70% sensitivity and 90% specificity for distinguishing PSC-CCA (n=17) and PSC alone (n=101).
